name: Greece Business Email List

decision_maker_profile.buying_authority: High — direct purchase authority typical at SME level

compliance.notes: Standard opt-out and sender identification required.

market_summary: Greece has a 10 million-person economy anchored by shipping (Greece controls the world's largest merchant shipping fleet by tonnage), tourism (25 million annual visitors), and a growing technology sector. Athens is the commercial capital with the majority of corporate headquarters, financial institutions, and professional services. Thessaloniki is Greece's 2nd city with significant manufacturing, trade, and university-connected technology activity. Greece has recovered significantly from the 2010-2018 debt crisis and is now growing again, with a particular surge in technology investment — Athens has emerged as a notable European startup hub with major investments from Uber, Microsoft, and global VCs.
strategy_note: Greek B2B cold email in Greek shows cultural respect but English is widely understood in business contexts, particularly in the shipping, tourism, and technology sectors. Greek business culture values personal relationships and trust built over time. The shipping industry in particular operates on tight relationship networks — Piraeus shipping circles are small and referral-based. Athens' technology sector is globally connected and more receptive to direct international outreach.
culture_note: Greek business culture is relationship-oriented, warm, and family-influenced — family-owned businesses dominate the SME landscape. Business relationships are personal and built around shared meals and social interaction (coffee, taverna dinners). Patience is required; Greeks are careful decision-makers who prefer long-term reliability over quick deals.
facts: Greece controls the world's largest merchant shipping fleet — 20%+ of global dry bulk capacity
Greek shipping generates $20+ billion in annual revenue for the Greek economy
Athens 'Riviera' tech corridor (Hellinikon) attracted 7 billion euros in tech investment
Greece is Europe's top summer tourism destination by Mediterranean arrivals
Thessaloniki hosts one of Europe's largest annual international trade fairs
industry_note: Maritime & Shipping is globally dominant — concentrated in Piraeus, the world's busiest Mediterranean port. Tourism & Hospitality is the largest employment sector. Technology is growing rapidly in Athens' emerging tech corridor. Finance & Banking is significant in Athens. Agriculture (olive oil, wine, feta) generates significant exports.
